channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Olievenhoutbosch residents deliver memorandum to Tshwane mayor over years of stalled housing and service delivery issues
item.description: On Tuesday, residents of Olievenhoutbosch marched to the Tshwane mayor’s office to hand over a memorandum addressing what they described as more than 11 years of neglect and ongoing failures in servic...

item.title: Analysis of UK Government Legislative Agenda for 2026 Parliamentary Session
item.description: The 2026 State Opening of Parliament outlined the government's legislative priorities for the new session, focusing on cost-of-living relief, EU relations, and various domestic reforms. Measures to al...

item.title: New Research Reveals Remoras Can Exhibit Parasitic Behavior in Manta Rays
item.description: Remoras, often considered harmless hitchhikers in the ocean, have been observed engaging in surprising and potentially harmful behavior toward their hosts. Traditionally seen as mutually beneficial co...

item.title: Trump States He Does Not Consider Americans' Finances in Decision-Making, GOP Lawmakers Hesitate to Respond
item.description: On May 13, 2026, President Donald Trump made a striking statement on the White House lawn, admitting that he does not consider the financial situations of Americans when making major policy decisions....

item.title: European air fares set to rise due to high jet fuel costs
item.description: Air fares in Europe are expected to rise steadily due to surging jet fuel prices, according to Willie Walsh, head of the International Air Transport Association. While some airlines have temporarily r...

item.title: Federal Court to rule on Coles’ Down Down discount practices
item.description: The Federal Court is set to deliver a significant judgment in a case brought by the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) against Coles over alleged misleading ‘Down Down’ discounts. T...

item.title: Indigenous-led Group Considers Future Pipeline Projects Amid Federal Retention of Trans Mountain
item.description: The chief executive officer of the Western Indigenous Pipeline Group (WIPG), Joe Dion, has indicated that the Indigenous-led company may have to adjust its plans to acquire full ownership of the Trans...

item.title: Special Non-Working Holidays Declared in Gattaran, Cagayan and Calaca, Batangas
item.description: Malacañang has officially announced special non-working holidays in two areas of Luzon to let locals participate in important community events. Proclamation 1242 sets May 20 as a special non-working d...

item.title: Quantum-Inspired Algorithm Enables Rapid Simulation of Complex Materials
item.description: Researchers at Aalto University have developed a quantum-inspired algorithm capable of simulating extremely complex quantum materials, such as quasicrystals, in seconds—a task that overwhelms conventi...

item.title: King Charles delivers the Queen’s Speech amid Labour leadership uncertainty
item.description: King Charles III delivered the Queen’s Speech at the State Opening of Parliament amid growing speculation over Prime Minister Keir Starmer's future. The article presents a satirical view of the procee...

item.title: Bpifrance Highlights Rise of Specialized SMEs and Local Partnerships in Africa
item.description: As many big French companies scale down their operations in parts of Africa, Bpifrance is seeing a shift in how French commerce works on the continent. Arnaud Floris, the Africa director at Bpifrance,...

item.title: Nairobi Hospital Celebrates Nurses for Their Dedication During International Nurses Week 2026
item.description: The Nairobi Hospital in Kenya celebrated International Nurses Week 2026 to honour the dedication, sacrifice, and vital role of nurses in providing quality healthcare nationwide. Organised by the Natio...

item.title: Tswaing Municipality Challenges MEC's Report on Maladministration in Court
item.description: The Tswaing Local Municipality in North West has taken the controversial step of approaching the high court in Mahikeng to contest a damning report highlighting serious maladministration and corruptio...

item.title: Fadiel Adams' Bail Application Faces Challenges Over Social Media Conduct
item.description: The Pinetown Magistrate’s Court is currently hearing the bail application of Fadiel Adams, leader of the National Coloured Congress and Member of Parliament, who faces multiple criminal charges includ...

item.title: Bangla Pokkho chief Garga Chatterjee held for three days by police over EVM claims
item.description: In Kolkata, Garga Chatterjee, the head of pro-Bengali group Bangla Pokkho, has been sent to police custody for three days until May 16. This action follows his arrest by the Kolkata Police, who accuse...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte expresses concern that Charter change discussions may extend Marcos' term
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte expressed concern over the growing talks of Charter change in the Philippines, warning that it could allow President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. to stay in power beyond...

item.title: The Story of Little Joe, the Canary Who Warned Miners of Danger
item.description: In the 19th century, canaries were not just pets but vital early warning systems for coal miners. Their sensitive respiratory systems made them ideal for detecting dangerous gases such as carbon monox...

item.title: Orlando Pirates on Verge of Premiership Glory After Sundowns Slip
item.description: Orlando Pirates are on the cusp of ending a 13-year Premiership drought as they prepare to face Durban City at home on Saturday. A single win will see them finish above Mamelodi Sundowns, who recently...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte expresses concern over disorderly Senate proceedings
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has expressed her concern regarding recent chaotic events in the Philippine Senate. While details of the incidents were not extensively reported, the Vice President highlig...

item.title: List of civil cases scheduled at Albury District Court on 13 May
item.description: The Herald Sun published a listing of all individuals scheduled to appear in civil matters at the Albury District Court on Wednesday, 13 May. The article aims to provide a public record of court proce...
